compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
`run_git` (`src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py:26-61`) is characterized only through callers that
monkeypatch it (`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_git_adapter_characterization.py:48-57`);
the real wrapper's own branches are never exercised directly. Signature:
`run_git(cwd, *args, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=None, env=None,
input_data=None)`. VALIDATED.

Separately, a latent contract hole was confirmed by direct probe: calling
`run_git(..., input_data=b"...")` with the default `text=True` raises a confusing
`AttributeError: 'bytes' object has no attribute 'encode'` from deep inside `subprocess`,
even though the signature advertises `input_data: str | bytes | None`. The wrapper should
turn this unsupported combination into a clear, actionable error rather than an opaque
stdlib AttributeError.

## What
1. Add a focused contract test matrix in a NEW file `tests/unit/test_run_git_wrapper.py` that
   invokes the REAL `run_git` (no monkeypatching of the wrapper) against a throwaway git repo
   created in a tmp dir, asserting each documented branch (see AC).
2. Narrow the `input_data` contract in `run_git`: when `input_data` is `bytes` and `text=True`
   (the default), raise a clear `TypeError` naming the fix (binary stdin requires `text=False`),
   instead of letting subprocess raise the opaque `AttributeError`. Update the docstring to
   state the narrowed contract. `str` stdin with `text=True` (the only combination any current
   caller uses) is unchanged.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py` — add the `bytes` + `text=True` guard in `run_git`; document it.
- `tests/unit/test_run_git_wrapper.py` — new direct contract matrix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cwd=None` omits the `-C` prefix (argv starts `git <args>`); a path-like `cwd` inserts
      `-C <cwd>` (argv starts `git -C <cwd> <args>`) — asserted by observing behavior against a
      real repo (e.g. `rev-parse --show-toplevel` resolves to the given repo only when `-C` is
      applied).
- [ ] `check=True` on a failing git command raises `subprocess.CalledProcessError`; `check=False`
      returns a `CompletedProcess` with the non-zero `returncode` instead.
- [ ] `timeout` on a command that exceeds it propagates `subprocess.TimeoutExpired`.
- [ ] `env` fully REPLACES the environment (a var present in the parent but absent from `env`
      is not visible to git), asserted via a git command that reads an env var.
- [ ] `input_data` (str) is forwarded to git's stdin (e.g. `hash-object --stdin` returns the
      hash of the piped content).
- [ ] `input_data=bytes` with `text=True` raises a clear `TypeError` (the narrowed contract),
      NOT the opaque stdlib `AttributeError`; a test pins this behavior.
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: new direct contract matrix for run_git (cwd/-C, check, timeout, env, stdin)
run_git wrapper under test; possibly narrow bytes+text type contract

compiled_state.title: Add a direct contract test matrix for the shared run_git wrapper
